title: Logout After Login Destroys Session and Prevents Back-Button Access to Protected Pages
description: Verifies the complete login→use→logout lifecycle: after an explicit logout, the session is fully invalidated server-side such that pressing the browser back button to a previously visited protected page does not re-grant access. This catches the common bug where logout clears client state but the server still honors the old session token.
expected_result: PASS: After logout, navigating directly to /dashboard redirects the user to /login. The authenticated UI is never shown with a stale session. FAIL: Navigating back to /dashboard after logout shows protected content (session not fully invalidated), or the page hangs/errors rather than cleanly redirecting to login.

test_steps.action: Wait for the dashboard to fully render with authenticated content
Click the user menu to reveal the logout option
test_steps.expected: Dropdown menu with logout option appears
User is redirected to /login and no session cookies remain

test_steps.action: Use browser back navigation to attempt to return to /dashboard
test_steps.expected: Browser navigates to /dashboard URL, but the app should redirect back to /login because session is invalid

test_steps.action: Confirm the login form is shown, not cached dashboard content
test_steps.expected: Login email input is visible — user is on the login page, not the dashboard
